text.title: Sunset Failed Product Playbook Core Rules
text.summary: 5 testable rules every Sunset Failed Product Playbook application must enforce. These are HARD rules — user-supplied input cannot override them.

rule.statement: ≥30 days notice before shutdown. Email per customer + in-app banner + status page.
rule.rationale: Surprise shutdowns destroy trust + invite chargebacks; 30 days is the floor.

rule.statement: Refund pro-rated unused subscription. Auto-process unless customer declines. Refunds are cheaper than chargebacks.
rule.rationale: Chargebacks damage merchant reputation; pro-rated refunds preserve it + are legally cleanest.

rule.statement: Provide one-click data export. Default = on; opt-out only. Customers should leave with their data.
rule.rationale: Locking customer data is the dark-pattern shutdown; export is the basic ethical requirement.

rule.statement: Recommend ≥1 alternative product (competitor or successor). Help customers land somewhere.
rule.rationale: Customers stranded mid-workflow churn the founder's reputation; recommendation is goodwill investment.

rule.statement: Publish a post-shutdown retro: what worked, what didn't, what next. Honest retros build founder reputation despite product failure.
rule.rationale: Audience-first founders' next product launches from the wreckage; retro is the bridge.
